# OpenCode

OpenCode is an open source AI coding agent that provides a powerful terminal user interface (TUI) for interacting with AI assistants to write, edit, and understand code. Built as a provider-agnostic alternative to Claude Code, OpenCode supports multiple AI providers including Claude, OpenAI, Google, and local models through a unified interface. The project is designed with a client/server architecture that enables headless operation and remote control from mobile apps or other clients.

The core functionality centers around sessions - interactive conversations with AI that can read files, execute commands, edit code, and perform complex multi-step development tasks. OpenCode features built-in LSP support, multiple agent modes (build for full development access, plan for read-only analysis), and extensibility through plugins, custom commands, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. Available as both a CLI tool and desktop application, OpenCode can be installed via npm, Homebrew, or other package managers across macOS, Windows, and Linux.

## SDK Installation and Basic Setup

The `@opencode-ai/sdk` package provides a TypeScript SDK for programmatically interacting with OpenCode. It includes both a client for connecting to existing servers and utilities to spawn and manage OpenCode server processes.

```typescript
import { createOpencode, createOpencodeClient, createOpencodeServer } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

// Option 1: Create both server and client together
const { client, server } = await createOpencode({
  hostname: "127.0.0.1",
  port: 4096,
  timeout: 5000,
  config: {
    logLevel: "info"
  }
})

// Use the client
const health = await client.global.health()
console.log(`Server version: ${health.version}, healthy: ${health.healthy}`)

// Clean up when done
server.close()

// Option 2: Connect to existing server
const remoteClient = createOpencodeClient({
  baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096"
})

// Option 3: Spawn server separately
const serverInstance = await createOpencodeServer({
  hostname: "127.0.0.1",
  port: 4096
})
console.log(`Server URL: ${serverInstance.url}`)
```

## CLI Installation and Usage

OpenCode provides multiple installation methods and a full-featured CLI for terminal-based AI coding assistance. The `run` command allows non-interactive execution with various options for model selection, session management, and output formatting.

```bash
# Install OpenCode
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash  # Quick install
npm i -g opencode-ai@latest                     # Via npm
brew install anomalyco/tap/opencode             # macOS/Linux (recommended)

# Basic usage - start TUI
opencode

# Run with a message (non-interactive)
opencode run "explain this codebase"

# Continue previous session
opencode run --continue "add error handling"

# Specify model and agent
opencode run --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514 --agent plan "review this code"

# Attach files to message
opencode run --file ./src/main.ts --file ./README.md "document these files"

# Fork session before continuing
opencode run --session ses_abc123 --fork "try alternative approach"

# Share session and get JSON output
opencode run --share --format json "fix the bug"

# Start headless server
opencode serve --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 4096

# Custom installation directory
OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

## Session Management API

Sessions are the core abstraction for conversations with AI assistants. Each session maintains message history, permissions, and state. Sessions can be created, forked, shared, and managed through the API.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// Create a new session
const session = await client.session.create({
  body: {
    title: "Bug Fix Session",
    permission: {
      mode: "default"  // or "acceptEdits", "manual"
    }
  }
})
console.log(`Created session: ${session.id}`)

// List all sessions with filters
const sessions = await client.session.list({
  query: {
    limit: 10,
    roots: true,  // Only root sessions (no forks)
    search: "bug"
  }
})

// Get session details
const details = await client.session.get({
  path: { sessionID: session.id }
})

// Get session messages
const messages = await client.session.messages({
  path: { sessionID: session.id },
  query: { limit: 50 }
})

// Fork a session at specific message
const forked = await client.session.fork({
  path: { sessionID: session.id },
  body: { messageID: "msg_abc123" }
})

// Share session to get public link
const shared = await client.session.share({
  path: { sessionID: session.id }
})
console.log(`Share URL: ${shared.share?.url}`)

// Update session title
await client.session.update({
  path: { sessionID: session.id },
  body: { title: "Completed Bug Fix" }
})

// Archive session
await client.session.update({
  path: { sessionID: session.id },
  body: { time: { archived: Date.now() } }
})

// Delete session
await client.session.delete({
  path: { sessionID: session.id }
})
```

## Sending Messages and Prompts

The prompt API allows sending messages to sessions and receiving AI responses. Messages can include text, files, and agent-specific instructions. Responses stream back with tool calls, file edits, and other parts.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// Send a text message
const response = await client.session.prompt({
  path: { sessionID: "ses_abc123" },
  body: {
    parts: [
      { type: "text", text: "Add input validation to the login function" }
    ],
    model: {
      providerID: "anthropic",
      modelID: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    },
    agent: "build"
  }
})

// Response contains message info and parts (tool calls, text, etc.)
console.log(`Message ID: ${response.info.id}`)
for (const part of response.parts) {
  if (part.type === "text") {
    console.log(`AI: ${part.text}`)
  } else if (part.type === "tool-invocation") {
    console.log(`Tool: ${part.tool} - ${part.state.status}`)
  }
}

// Send message with file attachment
const withFile = await client.session.prompt({
  path: { sessionID: "ses_abc123" },
  body: {
    parts: [
      { type: "text", text: "Review this file for security issues" },
      {
        type: "file",
        mime: "text/plain",
        url: "file:///path/to/code.ts",
        filename: "code.ts"
      }
    ]
  }
})

// Execute slash command
const commandResponse = await client.session.command({
  path: { sessionID: "ses_abc123" },
  body: {
    command: "init",
    arguments: ""
  }
})

// Get specific message by ID
const message = await client.session.message({
  path: {
    sessionID: "ses_abc123",
    messageID: "msg_xyz789"
  }
})

// Delete a message
await client.session.deleteMessage({
  path: {
    sessionID: "ses_abc123",
    messageID: "msg_xyz789"
  }
})

// Abort running session
await client.session.abort({
  path: { sessionID: "ses_abc123" }
})
```

## Server Health and Global Configuration

The global API provides server health checks, configuration management, and event streaming. Configuration can be set globally or per-project and controls model selection, permissions, and behavior.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// Check server health
const health = await client.global.health()
console.log(`Healthy: ${health.healthy}, Version: ${health.version}`)

// Get global configuration
const globalConfig = await client.global.config.get()
console.log(`Log level: ${globalConfig.logLevel}`)
console.log(`Default model: ${globalConfig.model}`)

// Update global configuration
await client.global.config.update({
  body: {
    logLevel: "debug",
    model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
    theme: "dark"
  }
})

// Get project-specific configuration
const config = await client.config.get({
  query: { directory: "/path/to/project" }
})

// Update project configuration
await client.config.update({
  query: { directory: "/path/to/project" },
  body: {
    model: "openai/gpt-4o",
    agent: {
      build: {
        tools: ["edit", "write", "bash", "glob", "grep"]
      }
    }
  }
})

// List available providers with models
const providers = await client.config.providers()
console.log(`Available providers: ${providers.providers.map(p => p.id).join(", ")}`)
console.log(`Default models:`, providers.default)

// Dispose all instances and cleanup
await client.global.dispose()
```

## Event Streaming API

OpenCode provides server-sent events (SSE) for real-time updates on sessions, messages, and tool executions. Events can be subscribed to globally or per-session.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// Subscribe to global events (SSE stream)
const eventStream = await client.global.event()

// Events include:
// - session.created, session.updated, session.deleted
// - message.created, message.updated, message.completed
// - part.created, part.updated (for tool invocations)
// - permission.requested (when AI needs approval)

// Subscribe to session-specific events
const sessionEvents = await client.event.subscribe({
  query: { directory: "/path/to/project" }
})

// Example event handling (pseudo-code for SSE processing)
// eventStream.on('message', (event) => {
//   const data = JSON.parse(event.data)
//   switch (data.type) {
//     case 'message.updated':
//       console.log(`Message ${data.messageID} updated`)
//       break
//     case 'part.created':
//       console.log(`New part: ${data.part.type}`)
//       break
//     case 'permission.requested':
//       console.log(`Permission needed: ${data.tool}`)
//       break
//   }
// })
```

## Provider and Authentication Management

OpenCode supports multiple AI providers with various authentication methods including API keys, OAuth, and well-known endpoints. Providers can be configured globally or per-project.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List all available providers
const providers = await client.provider.list()
console.log("All providers:", providers.all.map(p => `${p.id} (${p.name})`))
console.log("Connected:", providers.connected)
console.log("Default models:", providers.default)

// Get auth methods for providers
const authMethods = await client.provider.auth()
// Returns available auth methods per provider (API key, OAuth, etc.)

// Set API key authentication
await client.auth.set({
  path: { providerID: "anthropic" },
  body: {
    type: "api_key",
    token: "sk-ant-..."
  }
})

// Remove provider authentication
await client.auth.remove({
  path: { providerID: "openai" }
})

// Initiate OAuth flow for a provider
const authUrl = await client.provider.oauth.authorize({
  path: { providerID: "google" },
  body: { method: 0 }  // Index of auth method
})
console.log(`Authorize at: ${authUrl.url}`)

// Exchange OAuth callback
await client.provider.oauth.callback({
  path: { providerID: "google" },
  body: {
    code: "oauth_callback_code",
    state: authUrl.state
  }
})
```

## Project and Workspace Management

Projects represent codebases that OpenCode works with. Workspaces enable isolated work environments using git worktrees for parallel development tasks.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List all projects
const projects = await client.project.list()
for (const project of projects) {
  console.log(`${project.name}: ${project.path}`)
}

// Get current project
const current = await client.project.current({
  query: { directory: "/path/to/project" }
})
console.log(`Working in: ${current.name}`)

// Update project settings
await client.project.update({
  path: { projectID: current.id },
  body: {
    name: "My Project",
    icon: {
      color: "#FF5733",
      override: "rocket"
    },
    commands: {
      start: "npm install && npm run dev"  // Run on workspace creation
    }
  }
})

// List workspaces (git worktrees)
const workspaces = await client.experimental.workspace.list()

// Create new workspace
const workspace = await client.experimental.workspace.create({
  body: {
    type: "worktree",
    branch: "feature/new-feature",
    extra: null
  }
})

// Create git worktree
const worktree = await client.worktree.create({
  body: {
    branch: "fix/bug-123",
    baseBranch: "main"
  }
})

// List worktrees
const worktrees = await client.worktree.list()

// Reset worktree to base branch
await client.worktree.reset({
  body: { path: worktree.path }
})

// Remove worktree
await client.worktree.remove({
  body: { path: worktree.path }
})
```

## Permission and Question Handling

When the AI needs to perform sensitive operations (file edits, bash commands), it requests permission. The permission API allows programmatic approval or denial of these requests.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List pending permissions
const permissions = await client.permission.list()
for (const perm of permissions) {
  console.log(`Request ${perm.id}: ${perm.tool} - ${perm.description}`)
}

// Respond to permission request
await client.permission.reply({
  path: { requestID: "perm_abc123" },
  body: {
    reply: "once",  // "once" | "always" | "reject"
    message: "Approved for this operation only"
  }
})

// List pending questions from AI
const questions = await client.question.list()
for (const q of questions) {
  console.log(`Question: ${q.questions[0].question}`)
  console.log(`Options: ${q.questions[0].options.map(o => o.label).join(", ")}`)
}

// Reply to question
await client.question.reply({
  path: { requestID: "q_xyz789" },
  body: {
    answers: [
      { labels: ["TypeScript"], custom: null }  // Selected option(s)
    ]
  }
})

// Reject question
await client.question.reject({
  path: { requestID: "q_xyz789" }
})
```

## File Operations API

The file API provides read/write access to project files, status information, and search capabilities including glob patterns and grep.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List files in directory
const files = await client.file.list({
  query: {
    directory: "/path/to/project",
    path: "src"
  }
})

// Read file contents
const content = await client.file.read({
  query: {
    directory: "/path/to/project",
    path: "src/main.ts"
  }
})
console.log(content.content)

// Get file status (git status style)
const status = await client.file.status({
  query: { directory: "/path/to/project" }
})
for (const file of status.files) {
  console.log(`${file.status}: ${file.path}`)
}

// Search for files by pattern
const found = await client.find.files({
  query: {
    directory: "/path/to/project",
    pattern: "**/*.ts"
  }
})

// Search file contents
const matches = await client.find.text({
  query: {
    directory: "/path/to/project",
    pattern: "TODO:",
    glob: "*.ts"
  }
})

// Find symbols across codebase
const symbols = await client.find.symbols({
  query: {
    directory: "/path/to/project",
    query: "handleClick"
  }
})
```

## PTY (Pseudo-Terminal) Management

OpenCode can manage pseudo-terminal sessions for running shell commands and processes with full terminal emulation.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List active PTY sessions
const ptys = await client.pty.list()

// Create new PTY session
const pty = await client.pty.create({
  body: {
    command: "bash",
    args: ["--login"],
    cwd: "/path/to/project",
    title: "Dev Shell",
    env: {
      NODE_ENV: "development"
    }
  }
})
console.log(`Created PTY: ${pty.id}`)

// Get PTY details
const details = await client.pty.get({
  path: { ptyID: pty.id }
})

// Update PTY size
await client.pty.update({
  path: { ptyID: pty.id },
  body: {
    size: { rows: 24, cols: 80 }
  }
})

// Connect to PTY via WebSocket
// const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://localhost:4096/pty/${pty.id}/connect`)
// ws.onmessage = (event) => console.log(event.data)
// ws.send("ls -la\n")

// Remove PTY session
await client.pty.remove({
  path: { ptyID: pty.id }
})
```

## Tools and MCP Resources

OpenCode provides access to built-in tools and MCP (Model Context Protocol) resources from connected servers. Tools can be listed and their parameters inspected.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeClient } from "@opencode-ai/sdk"

const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: "http://localhost:4096" })

// List available tool IDs
const toolIds = await client.tool.ids()
console.log("Built-in tools:", toolIds.builtin)
console.log("Dynamic tools:", toolIds.dynamic)

// Get full tool definitions with schemas
const tools = await client.tool.list({
  query: {
    provider: "anthropic",
    model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
  }
})
for (const tool of tools.tools) {
  console.log(`${tool.name}: ${tool.description}`)
}

// List MCP resources
const resources = await client.experimental.resource.list()
for (const [name, resource] of Object.entries(resources)) {
  console.log(`Resource: ${name} - ${resource.description}`)
}

// List available commands
const commands = await client.command.list()
for (const cmd of commands) {
  console.log(`/${cmd.name}: ${cmd.description}`)
}
```

## Configuration File Format

OpenCode configuration can be specified in `opencode.json` or `opencode.jsonc` files at global (`~/.config/opencode/`) or project level. The `.opencode/` directory provides additional customization.

```jsonc
// opencode.json - Project configuration
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "logLevel": "info",
  "theme": "dark",

  // Agent configuration
  "agent": {
    "build": {
      "tools": ["edit", "write", "bash", "glob", "grep", "read", "webfetch"],
      "system": "You are a helpful coding assistant."
    },
    "plan": {
      "tools": ["read", "glob", "grep"],
      "system": "You are a code analysis assistant. Do not modify files."
    }
  },

  // Mode/permission presets
  "mode": {
    "acceptEdits": {
      "permission": {
        "edit": "always",
        "write": "always"
      }
    }
  },

  // Custom provider configuration
  "provider": {
    "custom-openai": {
      "api": "openai",
      "name": "Custom OpenAI",
      "env": ["CUSTOM_OPENAI_API_KEY"],
      "options": {
        "baseUrl": "https://custom.openai.com/v1"
      }
    }
  },

  // Plugins to load
  "plugin": [
    "@opencode/plugin-github",
    "./local-plugin.ts"
  ],

  // Custom instructions file paths
  "instructions": [
    ".opencode/instructions.md",
    "AGENTS.md"
  ],

  // MCP server configuration
  "mcp": {
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    }
  }
}
```

## TUI Launcher API

The SDK provides a function to launch the OpenCode TUI (Terminal User Interface) programmatically with specific options.

```typescript
import { createOpencodeTui } from "@opencode-ai/sdk/server"

// Launch TUI with options
const tui = createOpencodeTui({
  project: "/path/to/project",
  model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  session: "ses_abc123",  // Resume specific session
  agent: "build",
  config: {
    theme: "dark",
    logLevel: "info"
  }
})

// Close TUI when done
// tui.close()
```

## Summary

OpenCode serves as a comprehensive AI coding assistant platform that bridges the gap between powerful AI models and practical software development workflows. Its primary use cases include interactive code development through the TUI, automated code generation via the CLI, and programmatic integration through the SDK for building custom tools and workflows. The session-based architecture maintains conversation context across interactions, while the permission system ensures safe operation with file modifications and command execution.

Integration patterns typically involve either direct CLI usage for quick tasks (`opencode run "fix the tests"`), headless server deployment for remote access and automation, or SDK integration for building custom frontends and development tools. The provider-agnostic design allows teams to switch between AI providers without code changes, while the plugin and MCP systems enable extending functionality with custom tools, commands, and external service integrations. For enterprise deployments, managed configuration directories and well-known endpoints support centralized policy control across development teams.
